calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing For Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Fazio, In Propria Persona (510-590-7363) appears as self-represented petitioner/opposing party

Deputy Attorney General, Dane C. Barca, Ph.D. of the California Department of Justice (415-703-5605) appears as counsel for respondent/demurring and moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued within the context of the concurrent demurrer and is submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONER DAVID FAZIO'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is ordered OFF CALENDAR as moot in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer. The Court reads its finding and reason into the record as reflected in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter and as reflected in the clerk's .doc (Word) minutes. A written copy of the Court's ruling and findings is hand-delivered to each side in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)

Motion To Augment Record On Appeal (Dor Self-Employment Training Manual, April 06, 2015 Email, Petitioner'S Proposed Business Plan, U.S. Department Of Education Office Of Inspector General December 10, 2015 Audit Report); Mpa; Declaration
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

The motion to augment record is argued within the context of the demurrer and is submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

PETITIONER DAVID FAZIO'S Motion To Augment Record On Appeal (Dor Self-Employment Training Manual, April 06, 2015 Email, Petitioner'S Proposed Business Plan, U.S. Department Of Education Office Of Inspector General December 10, 2015 Audit Report) is ordered OFF CALENDAR as moot, in light of the Court's ruling on the demurrer. The Court reads its finding and reason into the record as reflected in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter and as reflected in the clerk's .doc (Word) minutes. A written copy of the Court's ruling and findings is hand-delivered to each side in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Fazio, In Propria Persona (510-590-7363) appears as self-represented petitioner/opposing party

Deputy Attorney General, Dane C. Barca, Ph.D. of the California Department of Justice (415-703-5605) appears as counsel for respondent/demurring and moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Real Party in Interest California Department of Rehabilitation's demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend. Requests for Judicial Notice are Denied. The Court reads its finding and reason into the record as reflected in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter and as reflected in the clerk's .doc (Word) minutes. A written copy of the Court's ruling and findings is hand-delivered to each side in open court. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
